lessons.title: Joint Tenancy and Tenancy in Common
lessons.description: Co-ownership arises whenever two or more people simultaneously hold an interest in the same piece of land. In Irish law, there are two forms of co-ownership: the joint tenancy and the tenancy in common.

lessons.title: Creation of Co Ownership under LCLRA 2009
lessons.description: We established that section 30 of the LCLRA 2009 creates a statutory default of joint tenancy for post-2009 conveyances to multiple parties.

lessons.title: Severance of Joint Tenancy
lessons.description: Severance is the process by which a joint tenancy is converted into a tenancy in common.

lessons.title: Rights and Obligations of Co Owners
lessons.description: The fundamental right of every co-owner — whether joint tenant or tenant in common — is the right to occupy the whole of the property.
